page: Microsoft Azure DS14_v2 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It supports AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets. The instance is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 229 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is consistently weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels (middle 50%) across stress-ng multi-core, geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth, both cached and uncached, remains in the average tier, with read-only large block speeds at 104.56 GB/sec and write speeds at 47.28 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model rated as poor (bottom 10%). Conversely, text generation for a small 135M model is strong, reaching 269.93 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS14_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7:1, positioning it as a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-thread compute performance. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable multi-core performance for parallel tasks. It is suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing, where its average multi-core capabilities and 112.0 GB memory capacity can be fully utilized without paying the premium for high-clock-speed compute-optimized hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure DS14_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. It features 112.0 GB of RAM (7.0 GB per core) and 229 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. However, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations scale to average levels. The instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small LLM models but struggles significantly with larger 70B models. Qualitatively, the DS14_v2 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads that rely on parallel execution rather than single-threaded speed. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS14_v2 is a DSv2-family VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112.0 GB of RAM, and 229 GB of local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and balanced multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 16 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
112.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
229 GB of local storage with no bundled GPU accelerators
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Weak single-core performance but strong small-model LLM text generation
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and parallel processing
